title: aerva radicans
intro: Aerva radicans, also known as the 'Kagdi Phali' or 'Kagdi Pat', is a type of plant species that belongs to the Amaranthaceae family. It is native to tropical regions, particularly in India and Southeast Asia, and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The medicinal properties of Aerva radicans include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-diabetic properties, which have been found to be beneficial in reducing fever, pain, and inflammation in the body. Aerva radicans can be used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, and is often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its numerous health benefits.
